name: Retatrutide
subtitle: Triple GLP-1/GIP/Glucagon Agonist | Weight Loss & Diabetes
overview: Novel triple hormone receptor agonist targeting GLP-1, GIP, and glucagon receptors. Phase II trials demonstrated 24.2% weight loss at 48 weeks—the highest recorded for obesity medications.
mechanism: Activates GLP-1 for appetite suppression, GIP for insulin sensitivity, and glucagon for increased energy expenditure and hepatic fat oxidation.
keyBenefits: Improved glycemic control (HbA1c reduction up to 2.16%)
Triple mechanism addresses obesity through multiple pathways

items.name: Superior Weight Reduction
items.description: Clinical trials show 17.5% weight loss at 24 weeks and 24.2% at 48 weeks.

items.name: Sustained Weight Management
items.description: Continuous weight loss with no plateau at 48 weeks suggests greater long-term potential.

items.name: Triple Mechanism
items.description: Addresses obesity through appetite suppression, energy expenditure, and metabolic efficiency.

items.name: Superior Glycemic Control
items.description: HbA1c reductions up to 2.16% with 82% achieving target levels below 6.5%.

items.name: Glucose-Dependent Regulation
items.description: Balanced glycemic control with minimal hypoglycemia risk.

items.name: Insulin Sensitivity
items.description: Marked improvements in sensitivity with potential for reduced exogenous insulin requirements.

items.name: Lipid Improvement
items.description: Non-HDL cholesterol reductions up to 26.9%, triglyceride reductions up to 40.6%.

items.name: Blood Pressure Optimization
items.description: Consistent decreases in systolic and diastolic blood pressure across trials.

items.name: Hepatic Fat Reduction
items.description: Up to 82% reduction in liver fat with normalization in 90% of participants.

deliveryMethods.overview: Weekly subcutaneous injection into abdomen, thigh, or upper arm with site rotation.

steps: Allow vial to reach room temperature (15-20 minutes)
Clean vial top with alcohol swab and air dry completely
Add calculated bacteriostatic water slowly down vial side
Gently swirl in circular motions—avoid vigorous shaking
Allow full dissolution (2-3 minutes); solution should be clear and colorless
Store reconstituted solution refrigerated at 2-8°C for up to 28 days

keyBenefits: Extended half-life enables consistent hormone regulation
Minimal systemic burden compared to daily dosing

interactions.notes: Do not combine with other dual/triple agonists—risk of severe hypoglycemia and excessive GI effects.
Do not combine—overlapping GLP-1 agonist mechanisms increase severe hypoglycemia risk.
Both cause significant GI effects. Not recommended without specialist supervision.
May significantly reduce insulin requirements. Monitor blood glucose and adjust insulin doses accordingly.
Safe combination tested in clinical trials. Different mechanisms work complementarily for glucose control.
Clinical trials included SGLT2 inhibitors with no safety concerns.
Safe combination; BPC-157 may provide GI protective benefits during retatrutide use.
Space oral contraceptives by 1 hour before retatrutide due to delayed gastric emptying.

timeline.effects: Initial appetite suppression and mild GI effects as body adapts to triple hormone activation
Noticeable food cravings reduction and portion size decrease; early weight loss (2-5%)
Significant appetite control and steady weight loss (5-10%); improved glucose control
Substantial weight reduction (10-18%) with enhanced energy expenditure
Major weight loss milestone (15-22%) with cardiovascular benefits and liver fat reduction
Maximum clinical efficacy (20-24.2%) with comprehensive metabolic improvements

common: Gastrointestinal effects (nausea, vomiting, diarrhea)—typically mild to moderate
Heart rate increases—common especially in first 24 weeks
stopSigns: Severe persistent nausea or vomiting preventing adequate nutrition
Signs of pancreatitis: severe abdominal pain radiating to back
Severe hypoglycemia symptoms: confusion, dizziness, sweating
Excessive weight loss (>3 lbs per week consistently or >25% total body weight)
Gallbladder problems: severe right upper abdominal pain
contraindications: Personal or family history of medullary thyroid carcinoma

good: Pharmaceutical-grade white powder with uniform texture
Clear, colorless reconstituted solution without particles
Stable extended half-life effects (consistent appetite suppression between doses)
warning: Source verification critical—counterfeit versions circulate due to investigational status
bad: Rapid tolerance or effectiveness loss suggests degraded or counterfeit product
Unusual side effect profile may indicate contamination

references.title: Phase II Obesity Trial
references.keyFindings: Landmark RCT in 338 participants showing dose-dependent weight loss; 12mg dose achieved 24.2% reduction—highest recorded for any obesity medication. NEJM.

references.title: Phase II Type 2 Diabetes Study
references.keyFindings: Trial in 281 participants with type 2 diabetes: 16.9% weight loss and HbA1c reductions up to 2.16%. The Lancet.

references.title: MASLD Substudy
references.keyFindings: Metabolic dysfunction-associated steatotic liver disease study: 82% liver fat reduction with normalization in >90% at highest doses. Nature Medicine.

references.title: Structural Insights into Triple Agonism
references.keyFindings: Cryo-EM structures reveal simultaneous activation of GLP-1R, GIPR, and GCGR, explaining superior clinical efficacy. Cell Discovery.
